commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
For thou. Hebrew, "Dost thou kill? "If those who have surrendered themselves in battle be often spared, though they might be slain by the strict laws of war, how much less ought these men to be treated with such severity? (Calmet)
Sicut bellantiresistenti violentia redditur: ita victo vel capto misericorida jam debetur. (St. Augustine, ep. 1. ad Bonif.) (Grotius)
And water, all necessary provisions. (Worthington)
These men were suffered to live that they might relate the wonders of God. (Theodoret, q. 20.)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
